name: emigration_intention
description: Measures the respondent's personal disposition and likelihood to emigrate from Mexico, capturing both attitudinal willingness and behavioral probability of moving abroad.

name: perceived_opportunity_deprivation
description: Captures the respondent's perception of limited structural opportunities in Mexico across economic and social domains, reflecting a sense of relative deprivation that may motivate emigration.

name: transnational_social_ties
description: Measures the strength and density of the respondent's social network connections to Mexicans living abroad, including family, friends, remittances, and frequency of contact.

name: xenophilia_tolerance
description: Assesses the respondent's openness toward foreigners and cultural diversity in Mexico, including attitudes about foreigners' rights, economic contributions, and treatment relative to Mexicans.

name: migration_policy_attitudes
description: Measures the respondent's normative orientations toward migration governance, including views on undocumented migration, government protection of migrants, voting rights abroad, and Central American transit migrants.
